title: feat(gen): add realtime voice/audio API (gpt-realtime-2)
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- New endpoint: `/v1/realtime` on `gen.pollinations.ai` — OpenAI-compatible realtime API for low-latency voice and multimodal sessions
- Single model: `gpt-realtime-2` (text + audio in/out, tools, reasoning)
- Drop-in for any OpenAI realtime SDK by pointing it at `gen.pollinations.ai`
- Registered in `shared/registry/realtime.ts` (paid-only) so pricing, model discovery, and billing reuse the existing pipeline
- Per-session billing settled on close: one `generate.realtime` Tinybird event with the summed usage
- Public proxy fix: WS 101 responses now pass through with their `webSocket` field intact (the proxy was dropping it, returning HTTP 500 at the edge)
- Credential query params (`key`, `token`, `api_key`) redacted in logger + observability captures — browsers must use `?key=` since `new WebSocket()` can't set headers
- 7 vitest cases cover proxy behavior, auth, model gating, aggregate billing, usage fallback, and `/v1/models` listing

## Test plan
- [x] `npm run test -- realtime docs error-observability` — 15/15 pass
- [x] `npx tsc --noEmit` clean
- [x] Proxy worker deployed to staging (`pollinations-proxy-staging` version `73bd8ff1`)
- [x] Gen worker deployed to staging (`pollinations-gen-staging` version `6924a9a0`)
- [x] End-to-end voice session through `wss://staging.gen.pollinations.ai/v1/realtime` with a paid BYOP key: real mic in, audio reply out, balance debits per registry rate

## Notes
- Pricing per token (multiplier 1.0): prompt audio 0.000032 / completion audio 0.000064 / prompt text 0.000004 / completion text 0.000024 / cached 0.0000004 / prompt image 0.000005
- Direct to OpenAI; Azure has `gpt-realtime-2` GA in 6 regions and the swap would be ~10 lines if we want to migrate later
- `?key=` accepts both `pk_` and `sk_`; recommend `pk_` for browser clients since URL query strings leak through referrers

gist.headline: Speak to the swarm
gist.blurb: Added the new /v1/realtime endpoint with gpt-realtime-2. Now you can build low-latency voice and multimodal apps just by pointing your OpenAI SDK at pollinations.
gist.summary: Added the /v1/realtime endpoint to gen.pollinations.ai, providing an OpenAI-compatible WebSocket connection for low-latency voice and multimodal sessions using gpt-realtime-2.
gist.impact: Developers can now build real-time voice applications by simply pointing any standard OpenAI realtime SDK at the pollinations endpoint.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee wearing headphones and speaking into a microphone, with the Monitor Robot displaying soundwaves, representing the new real-time voice and audio WebSocket API.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny studio headphones and speaking into a retro microphone. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby, its screen displaying glowing lime green (#ecf874) soundwaves. Soft pastel lavender and peach gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
